types.name: ID
types.description:
The `ID` scalar type represents a unique identifier, often used to
refetch an object or as key for a cache. The ID type appears in a JSON
response as a String; however, it is not intended to be human-readable.
When expected as an input type, any string (such as `"4"`) or integer
(such as `4`) input value will be accepted as an ID.

types.name: DateTime
types.description: A datetime string with format `Y-m-d H:i:s`, e.g. `2018-05-23 13:43:32`.

types.name: Float
types.description:
The `Float` scalar type represents signed double-precision fractional
values as specified by
[IEEE 754](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IEEE_floating_point).

types.name: Int
types.description:
The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric
values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.

fields.description: In cases where a podcast has multiple Apple Podcast IDs, this field will contain all of them (including the primary ID also defined by applePodcastsId)

fields.name: powerScore
fields.description:
The podcast's PowerScore, which is Podchaser's unique attribute that represents how popular and influential a podcast is. Calculated using a proprietary algorithm which uses dozens of different factors, such as reach and subcribers. Will return `null` when we do not have enough data for the podcast yet to generate a Power Score. Requires [our plus or pro plans](https://features.podchaser.com/api).

Only available with certain permissions.

fields.name: audienceEstimate
fields.description:
The estimated monthly listens the podcast receives for its RSS feed. Will return `null` when we do not have enough data yet for the podcast. Requires [our plus or pro plans](https://features.podchaser.com/api).

Only available with certain permissions.

fields.name: audienceEstimateRange
fields.description:
The estimated monthly listens range the podcast receives for its RSS feed. Will return `null` when we do not have enough data yet for the podcast. Requires [our plus or pro plans](https://features.podchaser.com/api).

Only available with certain permissions.

fields.name: episodeAudienceEstimate
fields.description:
The estimated number of listeners of an episode for this podcast's RSS feed. Will return `null` when we do not have enough data yet for the podcast. Requires [our plus or pro plans](https://features.podchaser.com/api).

Only available with certain permissions.

fields.name: youtubeAudienceEstimate
fields.description:
The estimated YouTube reach for this podcast. Will return `null` when we do not have enough data yet for the podcast. Requires [our plus or pro plans](https://features.podchaser.com/api).

Only available with certain permissions.

fields.name: totalAudienceEstimate
fields.description:
The total estimated monthly listens the podcast receives (including YouTube). Falls back to `audienceEstimate` when only RSS audience reach is available. Requires [our plus or pro plans](https://features.podchaser.com/api).

Only available with certain permissions.

fields.name: totalAudienceEstimateRange
fields.description:
The total estimated number of listeners of an episode for this podcast (including YouTube). Falls back to `audienceEstimateRange` when only RSS audience reach is available. Requires [our plus or pro plans](https://features.podchaser.com/api).

Only available with certain permissions.

fields.name: contacts
fields.description:
A list of contact information for people related to the podcast. Requires [our plus or pro plans](https://features.podchaser.com/api).

Only available with certain permissions.

fields.name: demographics
fields.description:
An aggregation of the demographics of the podcast's audience. Requires [our plus or pro plans](https://features.podchaser.com/api).

Only available with certain permissions.

fields.name: brandSafety
fields.description:
Average episode brand safety risk assessments for this podcast. 

Only available with certain permissions.

fields.name: networks

fields.name: politicalSkew

Only available with certain permissions.

The estimated reach for this episode from podcast-level data. Will return `null` when we do not have enough data yet for the episode. Requires [our plus or pro plans](https://features.podchaser.com/api). 

Only available with certain permissions.

fields.name: estimatedReach
fields.description:
EXPERIMENTAL BETA - the estimated number of listeners of this episode. Will return `null` when we do not have enough data yet for the episode. 

Only available with certain permissions.

fields.name: audienceReach
fields.description:
The number of listeners of this episode. Will return `null` when we do not have enough data yet for the episode. Requires [our plus or pro plans](https://features.podchaser.com/api). 

Only available with certain permissions.

fields.name: youtubeAudienceEstimate
fields.description:
The estimated YouTube reach for this episode. Will return `null` when we do not have enough data yet for the episode. Requires [our plus or pro plans](https://features.podchaser.com/api). 

Only available with certain permissions.

fields.name: totalAudienceEstimate
fields.description:
The total estimated number of listeners of this episode (including YouTube). Falls back to `audienceReach` when only RSS audience reach is available. Requires [our plus or pro plans](https://features.podchaser.com/api). 

Only available with certain permissions.

fields.name: totalAudienceEstimateRange
fields.description:
The total estimated number of listeners of this episode as a range (including YouTube). Falls back to `audienceReach` when only RSS audience reach is available. Requires [our plus or pro plans](https://features.podchaser.com/api). 

Only available with certain permissions.

fields.description: YouTube video mappings for this episode.

fields.description: Only available with certain permissions.

types.name: PodcastFilters
types.description: Filters that can be applied to a podcasts query.

inputFields.name: country
inputFields.description: Filter by the top XX podcasts per country (normally up to 500 per country). Expected in 2-character country code, such as us or ca.

inputFields.name: language
inputFields.description: Filter by what the podcast's feed states is the language of the podcast. Expected in 2-character language code, such as en or es.

inputFields.name: rating
inputFields.description: Filter by the average rating given by Podchaser user's for the podcast.

inputFields.name: categories
inputFields.description: Filter by the Podchaser categories attached to the podcast. Must search using the 'slug' of the category.

inputFields.name: addedDate
inputFields.description: Filter by the date that a podcast was added to the Podchaser database

inputFields.name: modifiedDate
inputFields.description: Filter by the date that any of the data within Podchaser was updated for a podcast.

inputFields.name: powerScore
inputFields.description: Filter by the podcast's PowerScore. Requires [our plus or pro plans](https://features.podchaser.com/api).

inputFields.name: identifiers
inputFields.description: Filter by specific podcast identifiers. Use Podchaser ID for best performance.

inputFields.name: dateOfFirstEpisode
inputFields.description: Filter by the date of the first episode released by this podcast

inputFields.name: dateOfLatestEpisode
inputFields.description: Filter by the date of the latest episode released by this podcast

inputFields.name: hasGuests
inputFields.description: Filter by whether the podcast is likely to have guests. True - has guests, false - unlikely to have guests.

inputFields.name: includeRemoved
inputFields.description: By default, removed podcasts are not returned in results. Set this filter to `true` to include both removed and active podcasts.

inputFields.name: audienceEstimate
inputFields.description: Filter by the estimated monthly listens the podcast receives for its RSS feed. Requires [our plus or pro plans](https://features.podchaser.com/api).

inputFields.name: episodeAudienceEstimate
inputFields.description: Filter by the estimated number of listeners of an episode. Requires [our plus or pro plans](https://features.podchaser.com/api).

inputFields.name: youtubeAudienceEstimate
inputFields.description: Filter by the estimated YouTube reach. Requires [our plus or pro plans](https://features.podchaser.com/api).

inputFields.name: totalAudienceEstimate
inputFields.description: Filter by the total estimated monthly listens (including YouTube). Requires [our plus or pro plans](https://features.podchaser.com/api).

inputFields.name: brandSafety
inputFields.description: Filter out (exclude) podcasts based on the podcasts's average episode brand safety risk assessments.

inputFields.name: status
inputFields.description: Filter by the Podcast Status

inputFields.name: socialFollowerCount
inputFields.description: Filter by the podcast's social media subscriber count, for a given set of social platforms.

inputFields.name: networks
inputFields.description: Filter by the podcast's network id or title.

inputFields.name: politicalSkew
inputFields.description: Filter by the political skew of the podcast. Requires [our plus or pro plans](https://features.podchaser.com/api).

inputFields.name: ageRanges
inputFields.description: Filter by age ranges of the podcast's audience. Requires [our plus or pro plans](https://features.podchaser.com/api).

inputFields.name: incomeRanges
inputFields.description: Filter by income ranges of the podcast's audience. Requires [our plus or pro plans](https://features.podchaser.com/api).

inputFields.name: genderBias
inputFields.description: Filter by gender bias of the podcast's audience. Requires [our plus or pro plans](https://features.podchaser.com/api).

inputFields.name: countries
inputFields.description: Filter by top countries of the podcast's audience. Can be either a country name or a two-letter country code. Requires [our plus or pro plans](https://features.podchaser.com/api). Accepts two-letter country codes (e.g., "US", "CA", "GB").

inputFields.name: states
inputFields.description: Filter by top states/provinces of the podcast's audience. Use the full state or province name. Requires [our plus or pro plans](https://features.podchaser.com/api).

inputFields.name: dmas
inputFields.description: Filter by top DMAs (Designated Market Areas) of the podcast's audience. Requires [our plus or pro plans](https://features.podchaser.com/api).

inputFields.name: occupations
inputFields.description: Filter by top occupations of the podcast's audience. Requires [our plus or pro plans](https://features.podchaser.com/api).

inputFields.name: interests
inputFields.description: Filter by top interests of the podcast's audience. Requires [our plus or pro plans](https://features.podchaser.com/api).

inputFields.name: ethnicities

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name and description, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields`, `interfaces`, and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: NON_NULL
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.name: QUERY
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.

enumValues.name: MUTATION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.

enumValues.name: FIELD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.

enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.

enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.

enumValues.name: SCHEMA
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.

enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.

enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.
